cases.title: Testing-plan §11 NFR-16 row — operational telemetry covers throughput, failure distribution, QR health, and session events
preconditions: Testing-plan §11 NFR verification matrix row for NFR-16 and §7 non-functional tests apply.
attendance-open-session fixture provides Open session with rotating QR, enrolled students, and mixed success/failure check-in attempts.
Harness can query structured application logs and operational aggregates (metrics sink or log-derived counters) for the test window.
steps: Map NFR-16 telemetry dimensions to testing-plan §11 minimum evidence: check-in throughput, failure reason distribution, QR rotation health, session open/close events.
Execute representative class-start window: lecturer opens session, students submit success and ExpiredQr failures, lecturer closes session.
Collect telemetry for each dimension: successful check-ins per minute, outcome code histogram, QrTokenIssued success/failure counts, SessionOpened/SessionClosed event logs.
Verify each dimension is observable with correlation identifiers suitable for class-time peak triage per 06-main-workflows §11.2 operational alerts.
cases.expected: Maps to testing-plan §11 NFR-16 minimum evidence: platform exposes metrics and logs for all four telemetry dimensions enabling issue triage during class-time peaks; operational drill artifacts document alerting and triage readiness per NFR-16 and AC-25 release checklist §4.2.

cases.title: Session service emits SessionOpened and SessionClosed operational logs with actor and timing at DB boundary
preconditions: Integration database spans M03 Session Lifecycle and structured logging/telemetry sink with real transaction commits.
Scheduled class session exists with assigned Lecturer; session state is Scheduled.
Harness captures domain event or structured log output for session lifecycle commands.
steps: Lecturer invokes POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open; capture correlationId and command latency.
Query structured logs or event bus for SessionOpened with classSessionId, actorUserId, openedAt, and initial roster count per 06-main-workflows §4.3 open-session telemetry.
Lecturer invokes POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close; capture correlationId.
Query structured logs for SessionClosed with classSessionId, actorUserId, closedAt, and close-processing metadata.
Verify class_sessions row state transitions match logged before/after states.
cases.expected: Session open and close commands each emit structured operational telemetry (SessionOpened, SessionClosed) with actor, ISO-8601 timestamps, session identity, and roster context per domain model §8.1 and NFR-16 session event visibility requirement.

cases.title: QR orchestrator QrTokenIssued telemetry records rotation health with TTL and issuance success
preconditions: Integration harness uses real qr_session_tokens persistence and M04 QR orchestrator with telemetry instrumentation.
Open class session exists with active token rotation scheduler (30-second TTL per BR-03).
Harness can read structured logs or metrics for qr_token_issued_total and token issuance latency.
steps: Invoke GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current and record token issuance success with expiresAt within TTL window.
Advance harness clock past TTL and re-invoke qr/current; verify new QrTokenIssued event with refreshed qrPayload.
Query telemetry for consecutive issuance success count, rotation interval, and any issuance error signals.
Simulate one ExpiredQr check-in attempt and verify failure increments outcome-specific counter toward QR health metrics per 06-main-workflows §6.5.
cases.expected: QrTokenIssued events and derived QR rotation health metrics report issuance success, TTL adherence, and ExpiredQr contribution to health signals; rotation remains observable for class-time triage without lecturer page reload per NFR-16 and AC-02.

cases.title: CheckInAttemptRecorded events feed failure reason distribution aggregates from persisted attempts
preconditions: Integration database contains check_in_attempts rows across multiple outcome codes (Success, ExpiredQr, NotEnrolled, DuplicateCheckIn).
M04 Check-in Processing and M08 analytics projection use real DB reads for outcome aggregation.
Known classSessionId anchors the aggregation window.
steps: Seed or trigger check-in attempts with distinct structured outcome codes for one Open session.
Query check_in_attempts grouped by outcome for the session time window.
Compare aggregate histogram to telemetry failure-reason distribution metric or structured log summary.
Verify Success outcomes are excluded from failure distribution while contributing to throughput counters separately.
cases.expected: Failure reason distribution telemetry matches persisted CheckInAttempt outcome histogram with 100% coverage of failed paths per AC-18; throughput and failure metrics are independently queryable for peak triage per NFR-16 and 09-error-handling §6.1 structured fields.

cases.title: Class-start concurrent burst on POST /v1/check-ins emits continuous check-in throughput metrics
preconditions: Multiple open class sessions each have 20+ enrolled students per performance-smoke profile.
Test harness fires parallel POST /v1/check-ins with distinct students, valid QR tokens, and unique Idempotency-Key values.
Metrics or structured logs capture check_in_success_total and request latency histogram during the burst window.
steps: Trigger simultaneous check-in submissions across concurrent open sessions mimicking class-start peak.
Record per-minute successful check-in count and median latency from telemetry sink for the burst duration.
Verify no gaps in throughput sampling during the peak window (every committed success is counted).
Correlate throughput metric with persisted Success CheckInAttempt row count for the same window.
cases.expected: Under class-start concurrency, check-in throughput metrics remain continuous and correlate with persisted success attempts; median latency telemetry supports NFR-01 triage without metric dropouts during peak load per NFR-16 and RSK-01 mitigation.

cases.title: Session state transition telemetry records beforeState and afterState for open and close commands
preconditions: Integration harness tracks session state machine per 07-state-machines.md.
Scheduled session exists; telemetry captures state transition events with beforeState/afterState fields.
Assigned Lecturer can execute open and close commands.
steps: Capture beforeState Scheduled; execute open command; verify afterState Open in telemetry and DB.
Record open command latency and token issuance success flag in same telemetry envelope.
Capture beforeState Open; execute close command; verify afterState Closed in telemetry and DB.
Query telemetry for illegal transition attempts (e.g., open on Closed) and confirm conflict events are logged without silent drops.
cases.expected: Each legal session transition emits operational telemetry with beforeState, afterState, actorUserId, and occurredAt; illegal transitions log conflict signals for triage per NFR-06 consistency and NFR-16 session event observability.

cases.title: HTTP contract — GET /v1/health returns operational status envelope for platform triage
preconditions: API service is running with postgres and redis dependencies healthy per docker-compose runtime checks.
ITAdmin or unauthenticated monitoring client can call GET /v1/health.
steps: Send GET /v1/health and assert HTTP 200 with standard response envelope.
Assert data.status is ok and dependency indicators (db, cache) report connected or healthy state.
Verify meta.requestId and meta.timestamp are present for log correlation.
Induce transient dependency degradation in test environment and re-query health; verify status reflects degraded state.
cases.expected: GET /v1/health returns HTTP 200 with documented health payload including service and dependency status per FR-33; health transitions are observable for class-time incident triage aligning with NFR-16 operational telemetry and 09-error-handling §7.1 Sev-1 detection.

cases.title: HTTP contract — POST /v1/check-ins emits structured log fields for correlation and failure triage
preconditions: Open class session with valid qrToken exists; enrolled student bearer token is available.
Log capture sink is enabled for API request handling (requestId, correlationId, endpoint, method, errorCode, httpStatus).
Test can trigger both Success and ExpiredQr terminal outcomes.
steps: Submit successful POST /v1/check-ins; capture response meta.requestId and response correlationId.
Query structured logs for the success path; verify requestId, correlationId, actorUserId, endpoint POST /v1/check-ins, httpStatus 200.
Submit failed POST /v1/check-ins with expired qrToken; capture error.code ExpiredQr.
Query structured logs for failure path; verify errorCode ExpiredQr, httpStatus 422, and scopeContext fields per 09-error-handling §6.1.
cases.expected: Every check-in command path produces structured operational logs with mandatory triage fields (requestId, correlationId, endpoint, method, errorCode, httpStatus); failure logs include stable outcome codes for distribution metrics per NFR-16 and AC-18.

cases.title: HTTP contract — GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance exposes failure distribution via counts.rejectedAttempts
preconditions: Open session with enrolled students; at least two ExpiredQr and one Success check-in attempts are persisted.
Assigned Lecturer bearer token is authenticated.
Roster projection reflects latest CheckInAttempt outcomes per FR-19.
steps: Lecturer calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance.
Assert HTTP 200 with data.counts.rejectedAttempts matching persisted failed attempt count.
Inspect data.rows[].latestAttemptOutcome for students with failures; verify outcome codes match CheckInAttempt records.
Trigger additional DuplicateCheckIn failure; re-query roster and assert rejectedAttempts increments.
cases.expected: Live roster HTTP contract exposes operational failure visibility through counts.rejectedAttempts and per-row latestAttemptOutcome aligned with persisted attempts; lecturers can triage class-time rejection spikes without audit-log queries per NFR-16 failure distribution and 06-main-workflows §6.5.

cases.title: WF-02 session open primary flow records open-session telemetry for QR rotation and roster baseline
preconditions: Scheduled session exists with assigned Lecturer per WF-02.
Telemetry sink captures open command latency, token issuance success, and initial enrolled roster count per 06-main-workflows §4.3.
Enrolled student count is known before open.
steps: Lecturer executes POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open with Idempotency-Key.
Verify HTTP 200, session state Open, and GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current returns Valid token.
Collect open-session telemetry: command latency, token issuance success=true, initial roster count equals enrollment count.
Confirm QrTokenIssued telemetry begins rotation cycle with 30-second TTL metadata.
cases.expected: Primary WF-02 open flow emits complete open-session telemetry bundle (latency, token issuance success, roster count) enabling class-start monitoring per NFR-16 and open-session telemetry note in 06-main-workflows §4.3.

cases.title: Flow C operational triage — ExpiredQr spike correlates with QR rotation health metrics after session open
preconditions: Open session with active QR rotation; multiple enrolled students available.
Incident playbook step 1 (detect via metrics/log alert) from 09-error-handling §7.2 is exercisable in test harness.
Harness can advance clock to force ExpiredQr without stopping rotation scheduler.
steps: Lecturer opens session; baseline QR health metrics show normal issuance success rate.
Students submit check-ins with deliberately stale qrToken tokens to generate ExpiredQr burst in first 2 minutes after open.
Query failure reason distribution for ExpiredQr spike and QR health degradation signal per 06-main-workflows §11.2 operational alerts.
ITAdmin or support operator traces correlationIds from structured logs to identify token staleness versus rotation failure.
Students rescan current QR; verify success rate recovery in throughput metrics.
cases.expected: Operational triage flow detects sudden ExpiredQr spike via NFR-16 telemetry, distinguishes QR display staleness from rotation service failure, and confirms recovery after fresh token scan per RSK-09 mitigation and AC-25 operability gate.

cases.title: Student denied operational telemetry surfaces — audit logs and cross-session roster metrics without leakage
preconditions: Open session with mixed success/failure attempts and elevated rejectedAttempts count.
Student bearer token is authenticated; AcademicAdmin token available as control.
Student lacks AuditLog.read and live roster scope beyond self.
steps: Student calls GET /v1/audit-logs?actionType=CheckInAttemptRecorded&classSessionId={sessionId}.
Student calls GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance for session they are not rostered to view as lecturer.
Student calls GET /v1/reports/attendance with institution-wide filters.
Inspect denial responses for partial throughput counts, failure histograms, or other students' outcome metadata.
AcademicAdmin calls same queries as authorized control.
cases.expected: Student receives HTTP 403 Forbidden on operational triage endpoints without disclosing session throughput, failure distribution, or cross-student attempt metadata; AcademicAdmin control returns scoped operational data per NFR-09 deny-by-default and NFR-16 RBAC boundary.

cases.title: QR token issuance failure surfaces degraded rotation health telemetry without silent open success
preconditions: Test harness can simulate QR issuance service error per 06-main-workflows §4.4 failure table.
Scheduled session exists; Lecturer is assigned.
Telemetry captures qr_token_issuance_error_total and session open command outcome.
steps: Induce QR issuance service failure during POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open or subsequent qr/current refresh.
Assert open command fails before committing Open or records explicit degraded-state alert per workflow failure table.
Query telemetry for issuance error counter increment and absence of false-positive healthy rotation signal.
Recover service; retry open and verify QrTokenIssued success restores healthy rotation health metric.
cases.expected: QR issuance boundary failure emits degraded QR rotation health telemetry and does not report false healthy rotation; recovery restores normal QrTokenIssued success metrics enabling operators to triage token service errors per NFR-16, RSK-09, and 06-main-workflows §4.4.

cases.title: Lecturer PG-06 live roster displays rejectedAttempts count and failure outcome badges for class-time triage
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop Chromium at PG-06 /lecturer/sessions/{sessionId}/roster.
Open session has persisted Success and ExpiredQr CheckInAttempt rows for enrolled students.
Roster summary strip exposes present, late, pending, and rejectedAttempts counts per FR-19.
steps: Navigate to PG-06 live roster for the Open session.
Verify summary strip displays rejectedAttempts count matching API data.counts.rejectedAttempts.
Locate student row with latest ExpiredQr failure; confirm latestAttemptOutcome badge renders with localized failure indicator.
Trigger additional student ExpiredQr attempt from harness; refresh roster and verify rejectedAttempts increments without full page reload.
Confirm successful check-in rows show Present/Late badges without inflating rejectedAttempts.
cases.expected: PG-06 browser surface exposes operational failure distribution and rejection volume for lecturer class-time triage aligned with NFR-16 failure reason visibility and 06-main-workflows §6.5 lecturer/admin visibility table.

cases.title: ITAdmin browser journey reviews platform health and PG-15 technical operational logs for incident triage
preconditions: ITAdmin is logged in on desktop browser.
Platform health view or GET /v1/health consumer page is reachable for ITAdmin role.
PG-15 /audit/logs contains recent technical audit entries (system_config, login_lock, infrastructure) per FLOW-13.
Recent session open and check-in activity generated operational log volume in test window.
steps: Navigate to ITAdmin platform health or system status view; verify service status and dependency health indicators render.
Navigate to PG-15 /audit/logs; apply technical actionType filters (system_config, infrastructure).
Set from/to date range for class-session window; verify technical operational entries list with actor, occurredAt, and actionType columns.
Confirm academic attendance mutation write controls are hidden and ITAdmin cannot trigger manual corrections from PG-15.
Verify filter and pagination toolbar state syncs with URL query params per PG-15 listing spec.
cases.expected: ITAdmin browser journey supports NFR-16 class-time incident triage via health status visibility and PG-15 technical operational log review with filter, sort, and pagination; role boundary prevents academic mutations per FR-33 and permissions matrix §5.5.

cases.title: Campus support operational drill verifies alerting and manual fallback telemetry during physical class session
preconditions: Incident response playbook for check-in degradation is documented per NFR-17.
Staging pilot uses real classroom with campus network and physical mobile devices per testing-plan §6.2.
External metrics/alerting stack (e.g., Grafana dashboards) is configured for production-like ops review.
Playwright MCP harness cannot reproduce full external alerting stack or physical peak-load telemetry.
steps: Run class-start session with physical student devices; monitor throughput and failure distribution dashboards during first 5 minutes.
Simulate network or GPS degradation; verify alert fires per 06-main-workflows §11.2 operational alert rules.
Lecturer applies manual fallback; support operator correlates telemetry spike with structured log correlationIds.
Document drill evidence per testing-plan §11 NFR-16/NFR-17 minimum evidence for release readiness §4.2.
cases.expected: Physical operational drill confirms NFR-16 telemetry and alerting enable class-time issue triage with rehearsed manual fallback per AC-25 and launch gate peak load rehearsal control #1.
